title: rootless podman containers stop after logging out / disconnecting ssh session
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

On a server I have a normal user that runs rootless podman containers. When exiting the ssh session the container would stop. The same behavior occurs when testing in a vm and logging out the user that runs the container. 

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Use podman version 3.4.0-dev at revision https://github.com/containers/podman/commit/40eeb7d3ba1186056df326349be984920c55dc1b or newer and have rebooted since installing (I found this to be needed for reproducing in the vm)

2. Start a container on one user (for my testing I used `podman run -d --rm -p 8080:80 httpd`)

3. Log out from the user / disconnect the ssh session

4. Log into another user (for example root) and check if the container is still running (using `htop` or in my example `curl localhost:8080` 

**Describe the results you received:**

After 5 - 10 seconds the container is not running anymore / does not respond to http requests anymore.

**Describe the results you expected:**

The container should keep running like in previous versions.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      3.4.0-dev
API Version:  3.4.0-dev
Go Version:   go1.17
Git Commit:   40eeb7d3ba1186056df326349be984920c55dc1b
Built:        Sun Oct 17 02:10:51 2021
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```

```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md)**

Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

Tested both on a pysical server with ssh and a gnome boxes / qemu virtual machine

comments.body: systemd will kill all processes in the user session, unless you set on lingering.  I have a feeling that this is causing the issue.
Lingering was disabled throughout my tests and I was able to find out the exact revision using git bisect. The behavior is reproducible

We've made no changes that I am aware of that would affect this. Are you certain this is not an issue with a more-recent systemd?

Regardless, I would not expect the behavior you are describing to work unless `enable-linger` was set for the user in question - it sounds like this behavior working previously could well be a bug.
Well if the behavior is not intended in the first place then this is not a regression. I will just enable lingering on my system
